CGAL::Graphics_scene.name: CGAL::Graphics_scene
CGAL::Graphics_scene.brief: The class Graphics_scene stores points, segments, triangles, rays, and lines.
CGAL::Graphics_scene.detailed: Elements can be added, possibly with associated colors. Non triangular faces can be directly added and are triangulated internally.

members.name: add_point
members.brief: adds the given point in the scene.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 .

members.name: add_point
members.brief: adds the given colored point in the scene.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 .

members.name: add_segment
members.brief: adds the given segment in the scene.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 .

members.brief: adds the given colored segment in the scene.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 .

members.name: add_ray
members.brief: adds the given ray in the scene: a half line starting from p and having v as direction.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 . KVector a model of Kernel::Vector_2 or Kernel::Vector_3 .

members.brief: adds the given colored ray in the scene: a half line starting from p and having v as direction.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 . KVector a model of Kernel::Vector_2 or Kernel::Vector_3 .

members.name: add_line
members.brief: adds the given line in the scene, defined by p and v as direction.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 . KVector a model of Kernel::Vector_2 or Kernel::Vector_3 .

members.name: add_line
members.brief: adds the given colored line in the scene, defined by p and v as direction.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 . KVector a model of Kernel::Vector_2 or Kernel::Vector_3 .

members.name: face_begin
members.brief: starts a new face.

members.name: face_begin
members.brief: starts a new colored face.

members.name: a_face_started
members.brief: return true iff a face is started.

members.brief: adds the given point in the current face.
members.detailed: a_face_started() KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 .

members.name: add_point_in_face
members.brief: adds the given point in the current face, having the vertex normal.
members.detailed: a_face_started() KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 . KVector a model of Kernel::Vector_2 or Kernel::Vector_3 .

members.name: face_end
members.brief: ends the current face.
members.detailed: a_face_started()

members.name: add_text
members.brief: adds the given text at the given position in the scene.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 .

members.name: add_text
members.brief: adds the given text at the given position in the scene.
members.detailed: KPoint a model of Kernel::Point_2 , Kernel::Point_3 , Kernel::WeightedPoint_2 or Kernel::WeightedPoint_3 .

members.brief: returns true iff the scene has no element.

members.brief: clears the scene, i.e., removes all points, segments, triangles, and text.

members.brief: returns the bounding box of all the elements in the scene.

members.name: is_two_dimensional
members.brief: returns true if the scene is in 2D, i.e., lies on the XY or XZ or YZ plane.

CGAL::Graphics_scene_options.name: CGAL::Graphics_scene_options
CGAL::Graphics_scene_options.brief: The class Graphics_scene_options is used to tune the way that the cells of a given data structure of CGAL are considered.
CGAL::Graphics_scene_options.detailed: The different std::function can be modified to change for example the behavior of the drawing. VolumeDescriptor can be void for data structures that do not represent volumes. This class is a model of GraphicsSceneOptions when VolumeDescriptor is void , or a model of GraphicsSceneOptionsWithVolumes otherwise ( VolumeDescriptor non void ). DS a data structure of CGAL. VertexDescriptor a descriptor of vertices of DS . EdgeDescriptor a descriptor of edges of DS . FaceDescriptor a descriptor of faces of DS . VolumeDescriptor a descriptor of volumes of DS . void by default. Is model of GraphicsSceneOptions or GraphicsSceneOptionsWithVolumes

CGAL::Qt::Basic_viewer.name: CGAL::Qt::Basic_viewer
CGAL::Qt::Basic_viewer.brief: The class Basic_viewer is a Qt widget based on QGLViewer that allows to visualize 3D elements: points, segments, triangles, rays and lines.
CGAL::Qt::Basic_viewer.detailed: This class stores a reference to a Graphics_scene . Elements are added through the scene. This class requires CGAL_Qt6 , and is only available if the macro CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER is defined. Linking with the cmake target CGAL::CGAL_Basic_viewer will link with CGAL_Qt6 and add the definition CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER . CGAL::QGLViewer is our internal fork of QGLViewer class which is A versatile 3D OpenGL viewer based on QOpenGLWidget .

members.name: Basic_viewer
members.brief: Constructor given a pointer on a QWidget (can be a nullptr ) and a Graphics_scene .
members.detailed: title will be the title of the window.

CGAL::Qt::QApplication_and_basic_viewer.name: CGAL::Qt::QApplication_and_basic_viewer
CGAL::Qt::QApplication_and_basic_viewer.brief: The class QApplication_and_basic_viewer regroups a Basic_viewer and Qt QApplication .
CGAL::Qt::QApplication_and_basic_viewer.detailed: The QApplication is created in the constructor, but started by the run() method. This allows for example users to modify the on_key_pressed method of the Basic_viewer to define their own behavior. This class requires CGAL_Qt6 , and is only available if the macro CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER is defined. Linking with the cmake target CGAL::CGAL_Basic_viewer will link with CGAL_Qt6 and add the definition CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER .

members.name: QApplication_and_basic_viewer
members.brief: Constructor given a Graphics_scene and possibly a title.

members.name: run
members.brief: runs the QApplication , i.e., open the Qt window. A call to this method is blocking, that is the program continues as soon as the user closes the window.

members.name: basic_viewer
members.brief: returns a reference to the Basic_viewer associated with this.

GraphicsSceneOptions.name: GraphicsSceneOptions
GraphicsSceneOptions.brief: The concept GraphicsSceneOptions defines data and methods used to tune the way that the cells of a given data structure of CGAL are considered for drawing or to be added into a graphics scene.
GraphicsSceneOptions.detailed: The different std::function can be modified to change for example the behavior of the drawing. Has models CGAL::Graphics_scene_options<DS, VertexDescriptor, EdgeDescriptor, FaceDescriptor>

members.name: DS
members.brief: A data structure of CGAL.

members.name: vertex_descriptor
members.brief: A descriptor of vertices of DS .

members.name: edge_descriptor
members.brief: A descriptor of edges of DS .

members.name: face_descriptor
members.brief: A descriptor of faces of DS .

members.name: ignore_vertex
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given vertex must be ignored, false otherwise.
members.detailed: Returns false by default.

members.name: ignore_edge
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given edge must be ignored, false otherwise.
members.detailed: Returns true by default.

members.name: ignore_face
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given face must be ignored, false otherwise.
members.detailed: Returns true by default.

members.name: is_vertex_colored
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given vertex is colored, false otherwise.
members.detailed: Returns false by default. For non colored vertices, this is the role of the user of a graphic scene to decide which color must be used (cf. for example Basic_viewer , vertices_mono_color ).

members.name: is_edge_colored
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given edge is colored, false otherwise.
members.detailed: For non colored edges, this is the role of the user of a graphic scene to decide which color must be used (cf. for example Basic_viewer , edges_mono_color ). Returns false by default.

members.name: is_face_colored
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given face is colored, false otherwise.
members.detailed: For non colored faces, this is the role of the user of a graphic scene to decide which color must be used (cf. for example Basic_viewer , faces_mono_color ). Returns false by default.

members.name: is_face_wireframe
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given face is in wireframe, false otherwise.
members.detailed: Returns false by default.

members.name: vertex_color
members.brief: std::function that returns the color of the given vertex.
members.detailed: nullptr by default.

members.name: edge_color
members.brief: std::function that returns the color of the given edge.
members.detailed: nullptr by default.

members.name: face_color
members.brief: std::function that returns the color of the given face.
members.detailed: nullptr by default.

members.name: ignore_all_vertices
members.brief: ignores all vertices when b is true ; otherwise ignores only vertices for which ignore_vertex() returns true .

members.name: ignore_all_edges
members.brief: ignores all edges when b is true ; otherwise ignores only edges for which ignore_edge() returns true .

members.name: ignore_all_faces
members.brief: ignores all faces when b is true ; otherwise ignores only faces for which ignore_face() returns true .

GraphicsSceneOptionsWithVolumes.name: GraphicsSceneOptionsWithVolumes
GraphicsSceneOptionsWithVolumes.brief: The concept GraphicsSceneOptionsWithVolumes extends the concept GraphicsSceneOptions to deal with data structures that represent volumes.
GraphicsSceneOptionsWithVolumes.detailed: Refines GraphicsSceneOptions Has models CGAL::Graphics_scene_options<DS, VertexDescriptor, EdgeDescriptor, FaceDescriptor, VolumeDescriptor>

members.name: volume_descriptor
members.brief: A descriptor of volumes of DS .

members.name: ignore_volume
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given volume must be ignored, false otherwise.
members.detailed: Returns false by default.

members.name: is_volume_colored
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given volume is colored, false otherwise.
members.detailed: For non colored volumes, this is the role of the user of a graphic scene to decide which color must be used (cf. for example Basic_viewer , faces_mono_color ). Returns false by default.

members.name: is_volume_wireframe
members.brief: std::function that returns true if the given volume is in wireframe, false otherwise.
members.detailed: Returns false by default.

members.name: volume_color
members.brief: std::function that returns the color of the given volume, i.e.
members.detailed: the color of all the faces of this volume. nullptr by default.

members.name: ignore_all_volumes
members.brief: ignores all volumes when b is true ; otherwise ignore only volumes for which ignore_volume() returns true .
